---
title: "Review attestation questionnaires"
date: last-modified
---

Review and approve attestations to verify model attributes and formally certify their accuracy at a specific point in time. 

As a reviewer, this process enables you to assess the model owner's attestation by reviewing details related to ownership, usage, performance, controls and documentation. You can provide feedback on the submitted information and formally sign off on the attestation using your user name — helping ensure an accountable and traceable audit trail.

::: {.attn}

## Prerequisites

- [x] {{< var link.login >}}
- [x] There are models registered in the inventory that require attestation. [^1]
- [x] Attestation has been set up and an attestation instance is in the [{{< fa clipboard >}} Ready For Review]{.bubble} state.[^2]
- [x] You are a [{{< fa circle-check >}} Validator]{.bubble} or assigned another role with sufficient permissions to review and approve attestations.[^3]

:::

## Review attestation questionnaires

### Before you begin

These steps apply to attestations that are in the [**{{< fa clipboard >}} Ready For Review**]{.bubble} state, to be completed by a person who holds [**{{< fa circle-checkmark >}} Validator**]{.bubble} role for the model at the time the attestation snapshot was taken.

### Steps 

When prompted on your dashboard:

1. Click **Open Attestations {{< fa arrow-right >}}**:

   ![Attestation banner prompting you to open attestations](attestation-banner.png){fig-alt="Attestation prompt with a link to the attestation labeelled 'Open Attestations'" .screenshot}


   Alternatively, you can also access attestations for models by clicking **{{< fa clipboard >}} Attestations** in these pages:

   - **{{< fa cubes >}} Inventory** — Shows models that require attestation.
   - **{{< fa triangle-exclamation >}} Findings** — Shows associated model findings.

2. In the **Attestations** sidebar on the right, click an attestation request in the [**{{< fa clipboard-check >}} Ready For Review**]{.bubble} state.

3. To review the notes documenting the attestation status changes: [View attestation activity](#view-attestation-activity)

4. Accept or reject the attestation:

   - **To accept**: Document the status change in the [notes]{.small-caps .b} field and click **Confirm**.

     The status changes to [**{{< fa clipboard >}} Reviewed**]{.bubble}.

   - **To reject**: Describe the changes needed and click **Confirm**.

     The status reverts to [{{< fa clipboard >}} In Progress]{.bubble} and notifies the model owner to revise and resubmit.

::: {.callout}
**Next:** [Sign off on the attestation](#sign-off-attestations).
:::

## Sign off attestations

### Before you begin

These steps apply to attestations that are in the [**{{< fa clipboard-check >}} Reviewed**]{.bubble} state, to be completed by a person who holds [**{{< fa circle-checkmark >}} Validator**]{.bubble} role for the model at the time the attestation snapshot was taken.

### Steps

Following your [review of attestation questionnaires](#review-attestation-questionnaires) or when prompted on your dashboard:

1. Click **Open Attestations {{< fa arrow-right >}}**

   Alternatively, you can also access attestations for models that you are the validator for by clicking **{{< fa clipboard >}} Attestations** in these pages:

   - **{{< fa cubes >}} Inventory**
   - **{{< fa triangle-exclamation >}} Findings**

2. In the **Attestations** sidebar on the right, click an attestation request in the [**{{< fa clipboard >}} Reviewed**]{.bubble} state.

3. Click **Sign Off**.

4. Document the status change to [**{{< fa clipboard >}} Signed Off**]{.bubble} in the [notes]{.small-caps .b} field and click **Confirm**.

After you sign off, the attestation becomes part of the history of the model. 

::: {.callout}
The attestation sidebar is persistent between findings and inventory views, making it easy to reference attestation information while reviewing other model details.
:::

::: {.callout}
Once an attestation is signed off, it becomes a permanent record and cannot be modified. The system documents who changed attestation statuses for audit purposes.
:::

<!-- FOOTNOTES -->

[^1]: [Register models in the inventory](/guide/model-inventory/register-models-in-inventory.qmd)

[^2]: [Complete attestation questionnaires](/guide/attestation/complete-attestation-questionnaires.qmd)

[^3]: [Manage permissions](/guide/configuration/manage-permissions.qmd)
